findings.title: Bridge owner can mint arbitrary unbacked tokens without consuming any burn record

best_agent_match.title: Bridge owner can mint arbitrary unbacked tokens with no proof of burn

findings.title: Ownership transfer or renounce leaves the previous owner with live `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` powers

best_agent_match.title: Ownership transfer leaves the previous owner with live `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` powers

findings.title: Transfers and burns depend on a mutable external farming contract and can be globally frozen

best_agent_match.title: Every transfer and burn depends on a mutable external farming contract and can be globally frozen

findings.title: Hardcoded maintenance routine confiscates balances from listed holder addresses

best_agent_match.title: Hardcoded maintenance function is a confiscation backdoor for listed holders

findings.title: `configure(true, ...)` is repeatable and leaves stale farming contracts permanently privileged while stranding the old farming reserve

best_agent_match.title: Every transfer and burn depends on a mutable external farming contract and can be globally frozen

rejected_candidates.title: Inverted logic in transfer validation allows any transfer
rejected_candidates.reason: The `require` predicate is wrong, but ERC777 still enforces `fromBalance >= amount` in `_move` and `_burn`, so over-balance transfers and burns revert rather than enabling theft.

rejected_candidates.title: Predictable farming fund address allows front-running
rejected_candidates.reason: The `_farmingFund` address being derivable from block data does not by itself give an attacker control over that address or a practical way to steal protocol funds.

rejected_candidates.title: UUPS test implementation has no upgrade authorization and is fully takeoverable
rejected_candidates.reason: The issue is confined to `contracts/test/Proxiable.sol`; without evidence that this test helper is part of a deployed production path, it is not a reportable protocol finding.

rejected_candidates.title: Fee calculation loses precision due to integer division order
rejected_candidates.reason: This is standard integer-rounding behavior in fee math and no realistic protocol-level loss, theft, or lockup path was substantiated.

rejected_candidates.title: Token burn occurs before state update
rejected_candidates.reason: If anything later reverted, the entire transaction would revert atomically and undo the burn; the cited mapping increment does not create an independent loss scenario.

rejected_candidates.title: External call without reentrancy guard
rejected_candidates.reason: `payFee` uses `transfer`, which forwards minimal gas, and there is no vulnerable post-call state to exploit; this is not a realistic reentrancy issue here.

rejected_candidates.title: Lack of access control on farming configuration
rejected_candidates.reason: The cited configuration is explicitly protected by `onlyOwner`, so this is expected governance authority rather than unintended privilege escalation.
